description: Section 7 (Cross-Role Analysis) shows 0.0s in substep timing and cross_role is null in latest_run.json. Likely swallowed exception in analysis/cross_role.py or PipelineState field not populating. Add explicit logging around cross_role_analysis() call in engine.py.
notes: Resolved Session 10 (Mar 1 2026): cross_role IS populated (6 roles ranked + narrative). 0.0s timing = pure Python running <1ms. 'null' was misread of truncated 2KB JSON preview. shared_gaps: 0 is a quality issue (verbose gap descriptions resist normalization) not a pipeline bug. No code change needed. CMF-027 unblocked.

description: Total time regressed from 427s baseline to 476.71s after Session 7. Agent 3 (role_comparator) at 265s is the bottleneck. Check whether parallel_workers: 3 is being read from tuning.yaml and passed to ThreadPoolExecutor — may have been dropped when wiring num_ctx options in Session 7.
notes: Resolved Session 10 (Mar 1 2026): parallel_workers IS correctly wired. Root cause: Ollama serializes requests by default (OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL=1). Fix: (1) reduced self_consistency_samples 3->2 in tuning.yaml (saves ~88s; Agent 3 ~265->~177s); (2) reduced reasoning_context_window 16384->8192 (prompts are ~4000-5500 tokens). For true parallel: set OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL=3 before Ollama server starts (documented in tuning.yaml).

description: The 1-year internship vs 2-year full-time toggle is only on the left sidebar — easy to miss. Move to main input area above the fold or add a visible callout so users don't accidentally run pipeline on wrong track.
notes: Resolved Session 10 (Mar 1 2026): removed from sidebar; added as st.radio(horizontal=True) in main area between file uploaders and WHY statement with dividers and help text explaining 65/35 vs 50/50 weighting difference.

feature: Role recommendation bias / unbiased role expansion
description: When run on non-builder profiles the engine recommended Government Digital Services. Unclear if LLM anchored on builder's requirements during PRD creation. Need visibility into the full role universe considered and a way to expand frame without introducing bias.
notes: Resolved Session 13 (Mar 2 2026): Full 20-role audit completed. Fixes applied: (1) CMF-034: Digital Transformation added to GovTech required_skills; (2) CMF-036: 2 healthcare-pivot roles added; (3) Technical Fluency promoted from preferred to required in technology-program-manager (clinical managers matched all 8 prior required skills via graph inference). Management Consultant and Operations Manager intentionally generic — correct by design. Systematic fix for expected_signal weighting tracked as CMF-035 (Should).

description: Auto-pull submissions from Tally form gD5Qll and route into CMF pipeline. Eliminates manual PDF upload step for real users. Workflow: pull new submissions -> auto-load resume + WHY -> prompt for LinkedIn PDF -> run pipeline -> save to runs/ with submission ID. Batch pull (not webhook). 15 form fields mapped. TallyContext threaded through all 4 agents. tally_intake.py + processed_submissions.json created.
notes: Verified 2026-03-02: --dry-run processes fixture end-to-end; live API returns 3 real submissions (Abhi Pradhan / Delzaan Sutaria / Amos Ng). Ollama must be running for full pipeline execution.

description: Show candidate which skills were extracted and by what method (alias / LLM / inferred / transfer_label / graph_inferred). Builds transparency and trust — users need to understand why they have gaps before they accept the analysis.
notes: Added/updated Skill Extraction Details expander in app.py with method grouping, counts, and transfer_label support.

description: LLM pass that reads raw resume text and generates 2-3 industry-agnostic labels per described experience. Addresses core problem: candidates describe transferable skills in domain-specific language that misses alias matching. E.g. 'led quarterly business reviews with C-suite' -> ['executive communication' 'stakeholder management' 'executive presence']. Tagged match_method: transfer_label.
notes: New skills.py substep: generate_transfer_labels(). Guard against hallucination with evidence-grounding constraint.

description: Users should be able to re-upload a revised resume and re-run pipeline without starting from scratch. Session state should persist WHY statement and role selection between re-uploads.
notes: Closely related to gap-based follow-up (CMF-009) — iteration loop implies: gap shown -> user refines resume -> re-run

description: Intermediate step after gap identification: ask users contextual questions about whether they actually have flagged skills and what evidence supports them. E.g. coding skill: 'Did you take a class? Build with it? Informal learning?' Competitive intelligence: 'Did you do it extensively in prior roles?' Link directly to the relevant role section in UI.
notes: Also allows UI clickable role links for direct navigation

Add missing skills/aliases to onet_skills.json and skill_aliases.json. Directly addresses CMF-004 root cause — 98 skills is too thin, causing false gaps for non-builder profiles.

Pulled 388 essential skills; deduped against 681 existing name/alias tokens. Result: 98 -> 468 skills (+370 esco- prefixed entries) + 1563 new alias entries in skill_aliases.json.

Add ~8 aliases: clinical reasoning / clinical decision making / differential diagnosis / clinical judgment / evidence-based medicine / diagnostic reasoning / medical problem solving / clinical analysis.
notes: Added 8 clinical-context aliases mapping to Critical Thinking in skill_aliases.json.

feature: Add medical experience aliases for Healthcare Domain Knowledge and Clinical Workflow Understanding
description: PM HealthTech lists Amos as missing PREFERRED skills for healthcare domain knowledge and clinical workflow — despite him being a practicing physician. Add aliases to skill_aliases.json: patient care / clinical practice / medical practice / physician experience → Healthcare Domain Knowledge; ward management / discharge planning / clinical operations / hospital operations / patient flow → Clinical Workflow Understanding.
notes: Added physician/clinical experience aliases for Healthcare Domain Knowledge and Clinical Workflow Understanding.

feature: Rename HIPAA Compliance to Healthcare Regulatory Compliance + add international aliases
description: HIPAA is US-specific. Amos practiced in Malaysia — creates a systematic false gap for non-US healthcare candidates. Rename preferred skill in role_taxonomy.json product-manager-healthcare entry to Healthcare Regulatory Compliance. Add aliases in skill_aliases.json: clinical compliance / clinical governance / medical regulatory / healthcare compliance / data protection in healthcare → Healthcare Regulatory Compliance.
notes: Affects any non-US healthcare candidate.

feature: Fix gap dedup logic — substring match on skill names
description: gap_analyzer.py lines 207-215: dedup checks g.description.lower() in matched_set. Gap descriptions are sentences not skill names so this never fires. Fix: also strip gaps where any matched skill name (len >= 8) appears as a substring in the gap description. Two confirmed false gaps from Amos run: Lacks Technical Fluency (matched_preferred for Tech PM) and Missing Agile Methodology (matched_preferred for PM EdTech). Add: and not any(m in g.description.lower() for m in matched_lower if len(m) >= 8).
notes: Updated gap dedup logic in agents/gap_analyzer.py to filter matched skill substrings (len>=8) in gap descriptions.

feature: Validate barrier conditions against candidate profile before including as gaps
description: barrier_conditions from role_taxonomy.json are used verbatim in evidence_source without verifying they apply to the candidate. Example: No experience managing multi-workstream initiatives appeared in Amos's Tech PM gap despite him managing multiple clinical departments. Fix: add a pre-check step in gap_analyzer prompt instructing the LLM to explicitly verify each barrier condition against the candidate profile before flagging it as a gap. If it cannot cite specific evidence of absence, the barrier must be omitted.
notes: Prompt engineering change required.

feature: Add Digital Transformation to GovTech required_skills to prevent over-scoring for domain-agnostic candidates
description: Government Digital Services ranks #1 for Amos (0.7 composite / strong) despite him having no public sector experience and no stated civic tech interest. Root cause: GovTech required_skills are all generic leadership skills (Stakeholder Management / Project Management / Communication / Cross-Functional Leadership / Change Management) that any clinical manager will match via graph inference. No domain-specific filter exists. Fix: add Digital Transformation or Public Sector Technology to required_skills. This drops Amos from 62.5% to ~37.5% required coverage. Broader issue: role was designed with builder's civic tech interest in mind.
notes: Added Digital Transformation to government-digital-services required_skills; added canonical skill to onet_skills.json.

description: expected_signals in role_taxonomy.json carry no structural weight — they only surface as soft market_signals gaps. For GovTech: Prior government/nonprofit/public sector experience and Experience with digital transformation are unmatched for Amos but don't reduce his overlap_score. Fix: compute expected_signal_coverage as a new field in skill_overlap.py using embedding match between candidate profile text and each expected_signal string. Feed as a penalty multiplier into overlap_score (tunable weight in tuning.yaml). Unmatched expected signals should reduce structural fit meaningfully.
notes: Requires adding expected_signal_coverage field to PipelineState and skill_overlaps output.

feature: Add healthcare-pivot roles to role taxonomy
description: The 18-role taxonomy was built for the builder's career search (tech / strategy / product / finance / govtech). No appropriate transition roles exist for a clinical-to-MBA pivot. Amos's actual best options are absent from the taxonomy entirely. Add at minimum: (1) Healthcare Technology Consulting — strategy + clinical domain + digital transformation; (2) Digital Health Strategy — health system transformation + product sense + clinical background. These are the roles Amos should be seeing as Win Now. Without them the engine produces a misleading picture for clinical candidates.
notes: Added two healthcare-pivot roles: healthcare-technology-consulting and digital-health-strategy.

feature: Expand role taxonomy from 20 to 80 roles with track and MBA flags
description: 20-role taxonomy was built for one candidate profile. Need 80 roles covering: internship vs FT tracks / MBA-specific vs general professional / Babson entrepreneurial track. Add functional_category + track + mba_track + babson_fit fields to all roles. Add 14 new canonical skills to onet_skills.json.
notes: Full spec in CODEX-REVIEW-QUEUE.md

description: Add build_decision_sprint() to output.py producing 4-section card: 90-day role bet (decision_score=0.45*fit+0.30*conf+0.25*(1-effort_norm)), top-2 skill closures by cross-role leverage, weekly execution loop, go/pivot checkpoint. No new LLM calls. render_decision_sprint() added to app.py. decision_sprint weights added to tuning.yaml.

description: Convert build_skill_graph() in matching/skill_graph.py to module-level cached singleton (_SKILL_GRAPH). Private _build_skill_graph() builder. get_skill_graph() public accessor. backward-compat build_skill_graph() wrapper preserved. engine.py + skills.py updated to use get_skill_graph().

description: Wrap synthesize_profile() and extract_motivation() in ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=2) when both inputs present. Serial fallback preserved when one input missing. Per-future error handling isolates failures.
notes: Merged PR #7 (2026-03-02). Per-future try/catch is better than spec — allows one agent to fail without killing the other.
